grant_title: Roadmap to Electrifying New Jersey’s Public Bus Fleet
eligible_applicant_type: Private sector entities (in partnership with eligible applicants)
geographic_restrictions: Primarily focused on New Jersey, with potential regional collaboration opportunities in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic.
project_area: Transportation electrification, specifically the transition of New Jersey’s public bus fleet to zero-emission electric buses, including infrastructure development, fleet replacement, and charging station installation.
demographic_requirements: Priority given to projects that benefit low-income communities, communities of color, and front-line workers who rely on public transit. Emphasis on reducing environmental and health disparities in overburdened neighborhoods.
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include state and local governments, public transit agencies (e.g., NJ Transit), nonprofit organizations, and private sector entities in partnership with eligible applicants. Projects must align with New Jersey’s goal of achieving a 100% zero-emission bus fleet by 2032, as outlined in the Electric Vehicle Law (N.J.S.A. 48:25-3). Priority is given to initiatives that address equity, reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and improve public health in disadvantaged communities.
